recommendedNextStep.label: Schedule 7-day caring contact
recommendedNextStep.rationale: High-risk patient with active safety plan; caring contacts within 7 days reduce repeat attempts.

recommendedNextStep.label: Administer ASQ or PHQ-9
recommendedNextStep.rationale: No suicide-risk screening on file in the past 12 months.

recommendedNextStep.label: Clarify risk with C-SSRS or ASQ
recommendedNextStep.rationale: PHQ-9 Item 9 endorsed; further suicide-risk assessment indicated.

recommendedNextStep.label: Complete brief suicide safety assessment
recommendedNextStep.rationale: Non-acute positive ASQ — BSSA or CAMS SSF-5 needed to disposition.

recommendedNextStep.label: Urgent face-to-face risk assessment
recommendedNextStep.rationale: Acute positive ASQ — do not leave alone. Initiate emergency safety procedures and clarify risk immediately.

recommendedNextStep.label: Complete Stanley-Brown Safety Plan
recommendedNextStep.rationale: Risk status set to high — safety plan and means counseling required before discharge.

recommendedNextStep.label: Confirm outpatient appointment scheduled
recommendedNextStep.rationale: Referral sent 10 days ago; outpatient receipt not yet confirmed. High-risk handoff window closing.

recommendedNextStep.label: Confirm no active-risk escalation; prepare for outcome reporting
recommendedNextStep.rationale: Caring-contact follow-up complete with no risk escalation; low risk sustained. Ready to close out the active-risk window and move toward Q2 outcome measurement.

recommendedNextStep.label: Session 5: re-rate SSF vitals and review drivers
recommendedNextStep.rationale: Active CAMS episode; psychological pain still elevated (3/5). Continue weekly sessions until resolution criteria met.

recommendedNextStep.label: Include in Q2 zero-suicide outcome report
recommendedNextStep.rationale: Resolved episode — eligible for pathway-completion measurement and population-level outcome reporting.

recommendedNextStep.label: Confirm outpatient BH appointment + start caring-contact cadence
recommendedNextStep.rationale: ED suicide-care episode (Scenario 11): 28-year-old presenting with severe insomnia and anxiety, chronic pain and recent job loss. Positive triage screen, safety plan and lethal-means counseling completed in the ED. Discharged to outpatient BH — confirm receipt of the transition-of-care packet and begin the 24–48h caring-contact cadence.
